name: Isham Jones
description: American bandleader, saxophonist, bassist and songwriter (1894–1956)
abstract: Isham Edgar Jones (January 31, 1894 – October 19, 1956) was an American bandleader, saxophonist, bassist and songwriter.

works_created.t: I'll See You in My Dreams
The One I Love (Belongs to Somebody Else)

about_quotes.speaker: Woody Herman
about_quotes.evidence: So I got this offer to go with Isham Jones... hired me... I was playing tenor with Isham... when Isham decided to retire in 1936... the guys in the Isham Jones band and myself.

about_quotes.speaker: Al Gallodoro
about_quotes.evidence: I was with the Jones band around four months... I recorded two things with Isham Jones.

about_quotes.speaker: Spiegle Willcox and Al Gallodoro
about_quotes.evidence: Ish hired me... that’s how I happened to be with the Isham Jones band until October
about_quotes.doc_title: Spiegle Willcox and Al Gallodoro interviewed by Monk Rowe, Norwich, New York, November 14, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Al Gallodoro
about_quotes.evidence: I played for Jones and he hired me... We toured all of New England with Jones.
about_quotes.doc_title: Al Gallodoro interviewed by Monk Rowe, Oneonta, New York, December 28, 2005

about_quotes.speaker: Loren M. "Tex" Brewster
about_quotes.evidence: what instrument did Isham Jones play? Isham Jones could play anything he was a good an excellent sax player an excellent piano player we had two brothers two of his brothers in the band with us Tom Jones and uh what was it uh I can't think of his first name but anyways two of Isha's uh brothers but

about_quotes.speaker: Johnny DeDroit
about_quotes.evidence: Remember Isham Jones? No. He was playing at the Rainbow Gardens down the street. ... Isham Jones wants to get a hold of me. But I'm important. The union had known it, they had thrown me out bodily.
